title: T1078 — Valid Accounts

metadata.tactic: Defense Evasion, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Initial Access

content:
Valid Accounts (T1078) describes adversaries using legitimate credentials to access systems. After brute force or credential theft, attackers may authenticate successfully and blend in with normal user activity.

Post-compromise indicators:
- Successful login immediately after failed attempts from same IP
- Login from unusual geography or ASN
- Off-hours access for privileged accounts
- New session on bastion or VPN followed by lateral movement

Investigation steps:
1. Verify whether the successful login was expected (change ticket, known admin)
2. Review MFA logs — was MFA satisfied or bypassed?
3. Check commands or processes spawned in the new session
4. Correlate with EDR alerts on the same host
5. Reset credentials and revoke active sessions if compromise is suspected

Severity: Successful admin login after brute force pattern should be treated as HIGH or CRITICAL until ruled out.
